For orders on Office objection as flag "A". 2. For hearing of main case. 25.07.2025. Mr. Mujahid Ali Jatoi, advocate for the Petitioner. Mr. Aftab Nek Muhammad Bhutto, Asst. A.G Mr. Sardar Ali Solangi, DPG a/w SIP Ali Dost on behalf of SSP, Larkana, SIP Abudl Hai PS WDM, INspt. Islamuddin SHO, Muhamamd pur odho on behalf of SSP, Jacobabad. --------------- SHO, Police Station Muhammadpur alongiwth other police officials are present and file respective statements, taken on record. In his statement SHO, has categorically denies to have custody of alleged detenues however, submits that in-laws of the petitioner including her brother in law are wanted in Crime No. 04/2025, offence under section 397 PPC registered at Police Station Muhammadpur Odho, therefore, they have conducted raids, but the Petitioner has maintained instant petition so that they may not perform their duties. He also annexed list of cases. The brother in law of petitioner Moula Bux is also absconder in the captioned crime hence, submits that petition may be disposed of by directing the Petitioner to produce the wanted accused/ her brother in law as well as relatives. The copies of the statements have been handed over to the Counsel for the petitioner who upon receipt of the same submits that police has replied evasively, two women and a boy aged about 14/15 years are missing since 3.5.2025, and still their whereabouts are not known hence they apprehends danger to their lives. Since liberty as well as life of the alleged detenues are involved in this case therefore, SSP, Jacobabad is directed to look into the matter personally and verify whether the contention raised by the Petitioner are true or the stance taken by the police.

For hearing of main case. 25.07.2025. Mr. Abdul Rehman Bhutto, advocate for the Petitioners. Mr. Aftab Nek Muhammad Bhutto, Asst. A.G --------------- After arguing the matter at some length learned counsel submits that Petitioner has submitted application dated 09.5.2025 through Courier No.UMS/Acct. No.5488/2025, and still it has not been decided by the Secretary, Works and Services Department, Govt. of Sindh. He therefore, submits that Petitioner would be satisfied and will not press this petition if direction may be given to the Secretary, Works and Services Department, Govt. of Sindh, to decide application of Petitioner, submitted by the Petitioner (Annexure-C page 27). Learned AAG when confronted with above proposal as well as record, he does not oppose such proposal. Accordingly, this petition is disposed of with direction to the Secretary, Works and Services Department, Govt. of Sindh to decide the said application after providing equal opportunity to all concerned under intimation to this Court, within three months. J U D G E J U D G E S-Ashfaq.

Accordingly, this petition is disposed of with direction to the Secretary, Works and Services Department, Govt. of Sindh to decide the said application after providing equal opportunity to all concerned under intimation to this Court, within three months. Mr. Iltaf Hussain Khokhar, Advocate a/w. petitioner. --------------- 1. Urgency is granted. 2. Office objection to be complied with within ten days. 3. Exemption is granted subject to all just exceptions. 4&5. It is the case of Petitioner that he applied for the post of Police Constable in Sindh Police Department and qualified physical as well as interview tests, conducted by SIBA under seat Number 10686 with flying colors. Thereafter, offer letter dated 23.10.2024, was issued to him however, he has not been issued joining order on the pretext that he was involved in a criminal case. Learned Counsel submits that petitioner who belongs to minority community has passed the tests and interview and he was issued offer letter too but he has been deprived of to get appointment due to his involvement in a criminal case though he was honourably acquitted, which is illegal and unlawful. In support of his contentions he relied on a Judgment passed in CP.No.D-704/2023 and CP.No.D-81/2024 dated 27.2.2025, wherein it is held by this Court that the candidate, if, acquitted and state /parties or the aggrieved persons have not assailed their acquittal, as such, their acquittal is “Honourable Acquittal”, and the case of present Petitioner is same, therefore, he being eligible to be considered for his appointment as Police Constable in Sindh Police Department.

U/A. 2. For orders on office objection as flag-A. 3. For orders on MA No.2066 of 2025. 4. For hearing of main case. 03.07.2025. Mr. Mazhar Ali Bhutto, Advocate for the Petitioner. -------------- 1. Urgency granted. 2. Deferred for the time being. 3. Exemption is granted subject to all just exceptions. 4. Learned Counsel submits that Respondent No.4 was tried and convicted by the Court of 1st. Additional Sessions Judge (MCTC), Larkana, vide Sessions case No.446/2021 (Re: State V. Ghulam Akbar Chandio), being outcome of Crime No.05/2021 registered at Police Station Seehar, under section 302, 114, 148, 149 PPC and was sentenced to life imprisonment in terms of Judgment dated 8.8.2024. Per Judgment custody of Respondent No.4 was given to Superintendent, Central Prison, Larkana however, later on he got himself transferred from Central Prison, Larkana to Central Prison, Karachi, where he has been allowed by the Jail authorities to move freely even beyond Jail premises. In support of his contentions he has annexed copies of Photographs and submits that Respondent No.4 has made viral TikTok video which apparently has been recorded at some Hotel of Karachi. Besides, he has been issuing threats that he would teach lesson to the Petitioner/complainant in case he may not settle score with him in terms of the compromise. Be that as it may, let notice be issued to Additional Advocate General, Sindh, Additional Prosecutor General, Sindh and the Respondents.

Besides, he has been issuing threats that he would teach lesson to the Petitioner/complainant in case he may not settle score with him in terms of the compromise. Be that as it may, let notice be issued to Additional Advocate General, Sindh, Additional Prosecutor General, Sindh and the Respondents. Meanwhile, office is directed to call comments from Respondents No.1 to 3. A copy of this Order shall be sent by Fax to Inspector General, Prisons, Sindh at Karachi. Besides, due process a copy of petition along-with annexures including original Photographs shall also be sent to I.G, Prisons, Sindh, for compliance. To come up on 10.07.2025. J U D G E J U D G E S-Ashfaq.

Mr. Abdul Rehman Bhutto, Advocate a/w Petitioner. Mr. Abdul Hamid Bhurgri, Additional Advocate General, Sindh. -------------- By Order dated 09.11.2021 instant petition, due to non-appearance of the Petitioner as well his Counsel was dismissed in default. However, by maintaining listed application being MA No.667/2022 which is fixed today for hearing. Counsel for the Petitioner seeks restoration of petition. The main prayer of the Petitioner was for appointment in Sindh Education Department, in terms of Project sponsored by UNDP in the year 2012. In case the listed application is allowed and matter is restored to its original stage as it was on 09.11.2021 even then main prayer cannot be exceeded to. Accordingly and in view of above, the listed application is hereby dismissed having become infructuous. J U D G E J U D G E S-Ashfaq.

Accordingly and in view of above, the listed application is hereby dismissed having become infructuous. J U D G E J U D G E S-Ashfaq.
